content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Bob S., Mike B., Ted                                      Date : 6/16/77

                                                               M-77-522

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Gina

Subject: Off-year report due July 31, 1977

1. An off-year report is due July 31, 1977 covering a period
   from January 1 through June 30, 1977.

2. The post election campaign statement due in connection with
   the special election being held in the 45th Assembly District
   is August 25, 1977.

3. The post election campaign statement due in connection with
   the special election being held in the 44th Assembly District
   is September 1, 1977.

If a committee makes post election contributions to both special
elections and has not had a previous obligation to file in either
election, an off-year report is due before the post election re-
ports. If the contributions are made prior to June 30, may the
filer combine the off-year and post election statements?

Question asked by Assemblyman Papan's office.

Gina advised him that he could. Technically he
might not be required to file the same statement at all since the election
ordered during the semi-annual filing period. The tougher question
is whether the semi-annual report should be waived when the
contribution is made after June 30 but before the closing date
for the postelection statement. Under a liberal reading at the
statute, such a contribution also would require waiver of the
same-annual filing requirement since the election for which the
contribution was made fell through during the semi-annual filing period.
cc: John K., Jay, John G., Alan, Nan

                                                               /30

sections.question:
asked by Assemblyman Papan's office.

Gina advised him that he could. Technically he might not be required to file the semi-annual at all since the election occurred during the semi-annual filing period. The tougher question is whether the semi-annual report should be waived when the contribution is made after June 30 but before the closing date for the postelection statement. Under a literal reading of the statute, such a contribution also would require waiver of the semi-annual filing requirement since the election for which the contribution was made still occurred during the semi-annual filing period.

cc: John K., Jay, John G., Alan, Nan

An off-year report is due July 31, 1977 covering a period from January 1 through June 30, 1977. 2. The post election campaign statement due in connection with the special election being held in the 46th Assembly District is August 25, 1977. 3. The post election campaign statement due in connection with the special election being held in the 44th Assembly District is September 1, 1977. If a committee makes post election contributions to both special elections and has not had a previous obligation to file in either election, an off-year report is due before the post election reports. If the contributions are made prior to June 30, may the filer combine the off-year and post election statements? Question asked by Assemblyman Papan's office. Gina advised him that he could. Technically he might not be required to file the semi-annual at all since the election occurred during the semi-annual filing period. The tougher question is whether the semi-annual report should be waived when the contribution is made after June 30 but before the closing date for the postelection statement. Under a literal reading of the statute, such a contribution also would require waiver of the semi-annual filing requirement since the election for which the contribution was made still occurred during the semi-annual filing period. cc: John K., Jay, John G., Alan, Nan
